author: y4rr
content:
Spun out of #591. That issue landed tensor-level send/recv (the existing all_to_all collective was already there) and a \`DeviceMesh\` for organizing ranks across multiple parallelism axes. The DTensor abstraction itself is a much bigger surface and needs a separate pass.

## What DTensor needs
A \`DTensor<T>\` represents a logical tensor sharded or replicated across the ranks of a \`DeviceMesh\`. The placement spec captures, for each mesh dim, whether the tensor is:
- **Replicated** (every rank in this mesh dim holds a full copy)
- **Sharded(d)** (split along tensor dim \`d\` across ranks in this mesh dim)
- **Partial(reduce_op)** (each rank holds an unreduced shard; the reduction is pending)

Operations on DTensor automatically insert collectives:
- Replicated → Sharded: scatter
- Sharded → Replicated: all_gather
- Partial → Replicated: all_reduce
- Sharded(d) → Sharded(e) where d != e: all_to_all

## Skeleton
\`\`\`rust
pub enum Placement {
    Replicate,
    Shard(usize),  // tensor dim
    Partial(ReduceOp),
}

pub struct DTensor<T: Float> {
    local: Tensor<T>,
    mesh: Arc<DeviceMesh>,
    placements: Vec<Placement>,  // one per mesh dim
    global_shape: Vec<usize>,
}
\`\`\`

## Scope
- Construction from a fully replicated source / from local shards
- Re-distribution (the placement transitions above)
- Pointwise ops via local tensor ops + same placement
- Reductions (sum, mean) — turn Sharded into Partial then redistribute
- Matmul: handled via partial sums on the inner dim
- Backward: each placement transition has a corresponding adjoint collective

## Why deferred from #591
DTensor ties together autograd, the collective layer, and the mesh layer in non-trivial ways. The torch implementation is ~3000 LOC of careful invariant management. Better to land it as a focused issue with a clean API surface rather than rush it as a tail of the p2p / mesh work.
